source.title: Hippocampal-Prefrontal Reactivation during Learning Is Stronger in Awake Compared with Sleep States

confidence_rationale: Direct empirical demonstration that hippocampal-prefrontal reactivation fidelity is context-dependent (higher in awake vs sleep SWRs) and is enhanced during initial learning. This matches MECH-269's prediction that anchor eligibility -- 'is this starting state trustworthy enough to roll forward from' -- should vary by context, with stronger anchored behavior during high-information moments (early learning, awake active behavior). Does not directly test per-stream verisimilitude but shows the state-dependent fidelity modulation the claim requires.
failure_signatures: If awake and sleep SWRs had equal reactivation fidelity, MECH-269's prediction that anchored-replay fidelity should vary with context-local model trust would lose empirical support.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Awake and sleep SWR reactivation differ profoundly: awake SWRs show higher-fidelity representation of behavioural experience, stronger CA1-PFC synchronisation, and are enhanced during early learning; sleep SWRs show lower-fidelity but integration-oriented reactivation.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-269 reads this as direct support for anchored vs probe replay as a context-differentiated phenomenon. Awake-with-high-CA1-PFC-synchrony ~= anchored mode (trusted starting state, writes to PFC viability map); sleep-with-lower-fidelity ~= more probe-like (exploration-seeded consolidation). The finding that awake SWR fidelity is highest during early learning matches anchoring on well-aligned streams when those streams are newly stabilised.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The awake-sleep axis is not identical to the anchor-probe axis. MECH-269's anchor/probe split is within-context, not across-context. Tang et al. report a different distinction (reactivation quality across major brain states) that is consistent with MECH-269 but not a direct test of it. A cleaner test would manipulate per-stream verisimilitude within a single state.
mapping.source_context: Male rats learning a spatial alternation task; simultaneous CA1 and PFC electrophysiology; awake behavior and subsequent sleep states.

confidence_components.notes: J Neurosci with rigorous simultaneous CA1-PFC recordings. Mapping is indirect: the awake/sleep distinction is consistent with anchor/probe differentiation but is not the specific claim.
